title: check-step command payload (the step's evaluated conditions)
description: The payload of a `check-step` journal entry (one base envelope + per-command payload — see journal.schema.json). It carries the flat list of condition results the harness evaluated for the step (mirroring the contract's conditions[]) plus the artifact refs the step read/produced and a pass/fail/skipped tally. The envelope carries orchestration/step/unit/actor/status; this payload carries the per-condition evidence. Formerly the standalone per-step log type (log.base.schema.json), now the check-step payload. Canonical term: 'log'.

outputs.description: Artifact references the step produced or edited.

counts.description: Tally of condition results for the step (pass / fail / skipped).

conditions.description: Every check the harness evaluated for this step, each a conditionResult mirroring the contract condition ({kind, type, expression, value, id}) plus its runtime result. Flat list, in evaluation order.

conditionResult.description: One check evaluation in a step's log line — the contract condition plus its runtime verdict. `kind` (precondition/postcondition/invariant), `type` (judgment category or structural after/input/output), `expression` (cel/instruction/ref) and `value` (the body) mirror the contract; `id` is set on every condition; `result` is the verdict; when skipped, `reason` says why; `detail` carries evidence.

kind.description: When the condition holds: precondition (before the step acts) / postcondition (after) / invariant (throughout).

type.description: What the condition is about: a judgment category (authority/content/clarification/challenge/consistency/transition/state) or a structural dependency (after/input/output).

id.description: Condition id, matching a steps[].conditions[].id token in the contract.

expression.description: The form of `value`: cel (harness-evaluated) / instruction (resolved structurally) / ref (structurally resolved).

value.description: The evaluated body: the CEL expression, the instruction URI, or the ref (artifact path / predecessor step id).

reason.description: When result is skipped: `unavailable` (an `after` predecessor checked with no `--log` run log supplied) or `logical` (a ref the harness cannot resolve).

detail.description: Optional evidence / explanation for the result (e.g. an attestation note).
